title: # Start Kernel Orchestration Start Kernel orchestration for the current task inside this Codex session. Execute immediately. Do not create or edit GitHub issues, pull requests, review comments, or issue comments. Do not inspect CI unless explicitly asked later. Backup-only GitHub Actions dispatch or...
summary_text: Findings only, focusing on post-preflight convergence flake: 1. **Hard timeout kill can convert near-success into `124` (highest-confidence flake).** In [run_with_pty.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/run_with_pty.py#L52), once `now >= deadline`, it immediately sends `SIGKILL` ([run_with_pty.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/run_with_pty.py#L54)) with no grace window. If guard/codex is about to exit cleanly around the deadline, this path forces timeout classification and canary fallback instead of fresh convergence. 2. **PTY tail capture is timing-sensitive and can truncate terminal lines near process end/kill.** Output is buffered and only emitted after loop completion ([run_with_pty.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/run_with_pty.py#L31), [run_with_pty.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/run_with_pty.py#L82)). Post-exit drain uses a very short poll window ([run_with_pty.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/run_with_pty.py#L69)). Around timeout/kill edges, final status lines can be lost from captured transcript, which presents as “ack missing” even when the child was close to completion. 3. **Bootstrap evidence timestamp is fixed before guarded launch, creating a freshness race under slow startup.** Evidence `created_at` is written during bootstrap summary ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L227)), then guard launch happens later ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L451)). If bootstrap/provider steps are slow, this increases risk that guard-side freshness checks reject otherwise valid evidence after preflight succeeds, causing convergence failure in runtime canary. 4. **Failure observability is coarse after preflight success (amplifies flake diagnosis cost).** The guarded command is executed as a bare command under `set -e` ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L2), [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L451)). Any non-zero exits before success marker printing ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L462)) collapse into generic missing-ack behavior at caller side, which makes intermittent convergence failures look identical even when root causes differ.
